title: Yacht Charter in Malta: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Malta, the sun-drenched archipelago in the central Mediterranean, offers some of Europe's finest cruising grounds for a yacht charter. Departing from the historic Grand Harbour in Valletta, you can expect weekly rates from €3,500 to €15,000 depending on the vessel. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Malta or list your own.

How much does it cost to charter a yacht in Malta?

Charter prices in Malta range from €2,500 for a bareboat week to over €15,000 for a crewed superyacht. Costs vary by boat size, age and season. A half-day shared tour on a rigid inflatable costs about €90 per person. For a more detailed view, you can compare options and book a charter online through BoatHire24, where many vessels are listed with transparent pricing.

Where to set off in Malta?

The main departure points include Portomaso Marina in St Julian's, the vibrant Sliema Creek, and the Malta Maritime Authority's berths at Msida. For southern routes, the small harbour of Marsaxlokk offers direct access to the Blue Grotto. Comino's Blue Lagoon is a must-visit anchorage, just 8 nautical miles from Valletta. Gozo's Mġarr Harbour provides an alternative starting point for exploring the north.

Which boat to rent in Malta?

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Princess V58), best for speed and luxury, up to 12 guests, from €5,000 per week. If you are interested in a particular model, have a look at this motor yacht available for charter.
  2. Sailing monohull, best for traditional cruising, 6-8 guests, from €2,500 per week bareboat.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), best for stability and space, up to 10 guests, from €4,000 per week bareboat.
  4. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), best for day trips and speed, up to 6 guests, from €500 half-day.
  5. Luxury crewed yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), best for all-inclusive service, up to 8 guests, from €10,000 per week.

Cost breakdown for yacht charter in Malta

  • Shared boat tour (half-day): from €80 per person, includes snorkelling at the Blue Lagoon.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, no licence): from €350 for a 4.5m boat for up to 5 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day charter: from €1,200 for up to 12 guests, plus fuel.
  • Crewed yacht weekly: from €8,000 for a 40ft vessel, including skipper and fuel.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Malta?

Yes, a recognised boating licence is required for bareboat charters of motor vessels over 10hp, but skippered charters require no licence. Malta generally accepts International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book. Season: high season runs from June to September, when the prevailing north-westerly wind (Mistral) brings steady breezes for sailing.

For the best experience in July and August, anchor early at the Blue Lagoon to secure a spot before the crowds; the water clarity is exceptional by 9am.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 (shared tour) to €15,000 (crewed superyacht) per week
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: half-day, full-day or weekly
  • Guest capacity: up to 12 on standard charters
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: required for bareboat; skippered available for all

Questions about boat rental in Malta

Can I charter a yacht in Malta without a licence?

Yes, you can hire a skipper with any yacht, or rent a small motorboat under 10hp without any licence.

What is the best time to sail around Malta in 2026?

The best months are May, June, September and October, when temperatures are mild and the wind is moderate.

How far is Gozo from Malta by boat?

The crossing from Valletta to Mġarr Harbour, Gozo, is about 12 nautical miles and takes roughly 70 minutes by motor yacht.

Are there any hidden costs in a yacht charter?

Be aware that fuel, port fees, and food for crewed charters are usually extra; check the listing details on BoatHire24.

Chartering a yacht in Malta gives you access to crystal-clear waters, historic harbours and secluded coves. Whether you choose a bareboat or a crewed vessel, planning ahead ensures the best choice. If you own a boat in Malta, you can browse the listings on BoatHire24 and become a host by listing your own vessel. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Malta or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Malta and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
